articles.title: Target, Best Buy, Costco, Macy's, Walmart and Amazon are part of Zacks Earnings Preview
articles.summary: Zacks.com released its earnings preview, highlighting several major retailers including Target, Best Buy, Costco, Macy's, Walmart, and Amazon. The report notes stable consumer spending driven by higher-income and younger groups, with a focus on essentials and experiences, while discretionary spending remains soft. It provides specific earnings and revenue expectations for Target and Best Buy, noting Target's recent stock momentum despite past underperformance, and Best Buy's modestly negative revisions trend.

articles.title: Jobs, CrowdStrike, Target, Broadcom, Costco, and More to Watch This Week
articles.summary: The S&P 500 index saw a slight decline of 0.4% last week, finishing February down 0.9%, marking its second monthly drop since April. This year, the benchmark index has traded within a narrow range, fluctuating between approximately 6800 and 7000. Investors are advised to watch companies like CrowdStrike, Target, Broadcom, and Costco, among others.

articles.title: Kimberly-Clark Corporation $KMB Shares Sold by Xponance Inc.
articles.summary: Xponance Inc. reduced its stake in Kimberly-Clark Corporation by 17.6% in the 3rd quarter, selling 12,829 shares and leaving them with 60,204 shares valued at $7.49 million. Kimberly-Clark recently increased its quarterly dividend to $1.28 per share, resulting in an annualized dividend of $5.12 and a yield of 4.6%. The company exceeded EPS estimates but reported revenue slightly below expectations, and analysts currently hold a consensus "Hold" rating with a target price of $119.00.

articles.title: Costco Wholesale Corporation $COST Holdings Lowered by Bedell Frazier Investment Counselling LLC
articles.summary: Bedell Frazier Investment Counselling LLC decreased its stake in Costco Wholesale Corporation (NASDAQ:COST) by 26.1% in Q3, selling 1,328 shares and retaining 3,753 shares valued at approximately $3.47 million. Despite this, Costco recently beat quarterly earnings estimates with $4.34 EPS and $67.31 billion revenue, and analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with price targets up to $1,185. Insider selling totaled 6,915 shares worth $6.32 million in the last quarter, while institutional investors hold 68.48% of the stock.

Despite this, analyst sentiment remains largely positive, with a "Moderate Buy" consensus and an average price target of $1,014.39. articles.title: Aster Capital Management DIFC Ltd Sells 1,065 Shares of Costco Wholesale Corporation $COST
articles.summary: Aster Capital Management DIFC Ltd significantly reduced its stake in Costco Wholesale Corporation, selling 1,065 shares and retaining 258 shares. This comes as Costco (NASDAQ:COST) reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations with an 8.3% revenue increase, and the stock is trading near $1,010. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with an average target price around $1,014, although company insiders have been net sellers, divesting a total of 6,915 shares in the past 90 days.

articles.title: Costco (COST) Gains Buy Rating From BofA on Strength of Pricing and Customer Loyalty
articles.summary: BofA Global Research has reinstated its "Buy" rating on Costco Wholesale Corporation (NASDAQ:COST), setting a price target of $1,185. The firm cites Costco's strong appeal among higher-income consumers, industry-leading pricing, and consistent dividend increases as key factors. Analysts believe Costco is well-positioned in the current economic environment, especially to benefit from tax stimulus.

articles.title: Target Corp (NY: TGT)
articles.summary: This article provides detailed stock information for Target Corp (NYSE: TGT), including its current price, trading volume, ranges, dividend yield, and performance metrics. articles.title: Costco stock closes above $1,000 as BofA restarts coverage ahead of earnings
articles.summary: Costco's stock closed above $1,000, up 2.44%, after Bank of America restarted coverage with a Buy rating and a $1,185 target. The retailer is preparing for its fiscal Q2 earnings and February sales update on March 5, with investors focusing on traffic, pricing, and membership fees. Despite a high valuation and lofty expectations, its strong position in a "K-shaped economy" and commitment to value pricing are key factors.

articles.title: Target Corp Announces Only Carrying Cereals Without Synthetic Colors
articles.summary: Target Corporation announced it will become one of the first national retailers to exclusively carry cereals made without certified synthetic colors. This change, driven by consumer demand for healthier options, is expected to be fully implemented by the end of May. The move aligns with Target's strategy to meet evolving consumer needs and establish merchandising authority.

articles.title: Zacks Industry Outlook Highlights Costco Wholesale, Ross Stores, Target and Dollar General
articles.summary: Zacks Equity Research discusses the Retail – Discount Stores industry, highlighting Costco Wholesale Corp. (COST), Ross Stores, Inc. (ROST), Target Corp. (TGT), and Dollar General Corp. (DG) as leading names. The industry benefits from consumers seeking value amid inflationary pressures, with key trends including resilient consumer spending, the search for bargains, enhanced omnichannel capabilities, and margin discipline. The article details the performance and outlook for each of the four highlighted companies.

articles.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Asset Management Co. Ltd. Increases Stock Holdings in Target Corporation $TGT
articles.summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Asset Management Co. Ltd. increased its holdings in Target Corporation by 2.9% during the third quarter, bringing its total to 952,942 shares valued at approximately $85.48 million. Other major institutional investors, such as Vanguard Group Inc. and Norges Bank, also significantly increased their stakes in Target, with institutional investors now holding about 79.73% of the company's stock. Despite mixed analyst sentiment with an average "Hold" rating, Target recently announced a $1.14 quarterly dividend, yielding 4.0%.

articles.title: Every Target cereal will ditch certified synthetic colors by May
articles.summary: Target announced that by May 2026, its entire cereal assortment, both in stores and online, will be made without certified synthetic colors. This change follows reformulations with national and owned-brand partners, aiming to maintain variety and price points. The initiative supports Target's broader focus on wellness, food innovation, and curated grocery experiences, building on the standards set by its Good & Gather brand.

The initiative supports Target's broader focus on wellness, food innovation, and curated grocery experiences, building on the standards set by its Good & Gather brand.

articles.title: Target Makes Leadership Changes To Boost Growth, Focuses On Style And Design
articles.summary: Target Corporation has announced significant leadership changes, including the appointment of Cara Sylvester as chief merchant, to accelerate its growth strategy and re-emphasize its focus on style and design. The retailer aims to enhance its merchandising capabilities, product development, and collaborations, drawing on Sylvester's expertise in digital, loyalty, and marketing. These moves, which also include Lisa Roath becoming chief operating officer, are intended to streamline operations, improve the shopping experience, and reaffirm Target's unique position in the retail market.

articles.title: Piper Sandler Trims Best Buy (BBY) Price Target, Maintains Neutral Rating
articles.summary: Piper Sandler recently lowered its price target for Best Buy (BBY) to $71 from $76, maintaining a Neutral rating due to expectations of normalizing home remodel demand and weaker store traffic. Wedbush also reduced its price objective to $70 from $80, citing downside risk to comparable sales after a soft holiday season for consumer electronics and ongoing memory supply shortages affecting PC demand. Both firms anticipate low single-digit declines in comparable sales for Best Buy, contrasting with current consensus forecasts for modest growth.

articles.title: Piper Sandler Trims Best Buy (BBY) Price Target, Maintains Neutral Rating
articles.summary: Piper Sandler and Wedbush have both recently lowered their price targets on Best Buy (BBY) while maintaining a Neutral rating, citing concerns over weaker store traffic, soft holiday consumer electronics sales, and ongoing memory supply shortages affecting PC demand. Both firms anticipate downside risk to comparable sales, with Wedbush expecting a low single-digit decline in contrast to current consensus expectations for modest growth. The outlook suggests limited near-term catalysts for significant upside.

articles.title: Target To Offer Cereals Made Without Certified Synthetic Colors From May
articles.summary: Target Corporation announced it will become one of the first national retailers to offer cereals made without certified synthetic colors starting in May. This move responds to customer insights and sales trends indicating a shift towards foods without artificial additives, especially for children's products. Target collaborated with national and owned brand partners to reformulate products while maintaining quality and value.

articles.title: More Retail Earnings Ahead: A Closer Look
articles.summary: The article provides an update on the retail sector's earnings, highlighting upcoming reports from major players like Target, Best Buy, and Costco. While overall consumer spending trends remain stable, driven by high-income and younger demographics, persistent inflation and soft demand for discretionary items continue to pose challenges. The Q4 earnings scorecard for retailers in the S&P 500 shows an elevated growth rate, though the proportion of companies beating EPS estimates is historically low.

articles.title: Wells Fargo Raises Target (TGT) Forecast as Company Focuses on Strategic Investments
articles.summary: Wells Fargo analyst Edward Kelly raised the price target for Target Corporation (TGT) to $130 from $115, maintaining an Overweight rating. This increase reflects Target's strategic investment plans and the analyst's belief that the new CEO can fund these improvements while delivering results, especially given the stock's 17% year-to-date gain. Additionally, Target announced it will stop selling cereals containing certified synthetic colors by the end of May, aligning with consumer demand for healthier options.

articles.title: Target Corp. Experiences Valuation Adjustment Amidst Competitive Retail Landscape
articles.summary: Target Corp. has seen its valuation adjusted, with its stock currently at $116.44 and experiencing an -8.60% return over the past year, underperforming the S&P 500. Despite this, the company maintains strong profitability with a P/E ratio of 12 and a robust dividend yield of 251.10%. Its valuation metrics suggest a favorable position compared to competitors like Walmart and Costco.

articles.title: Zacks Industry Outlook Highlights Costco Wholesale, Ross Stores, Target and Dollar General
articles.summary: The Retail – Discount Stores industry continues to thrive by focusing on value and efficiency, attracting consumers amidst inflationary pressures. Key trends include resilient consumer spending, a sustained search for bargains, enhanced omnichannel capabilities, and disciplined margin management. Zacks Investment Research highlights Costco Wholesale Corp., Ross Stores, Inc., Target Corp., and Dollar General Corp. as leading companies in this sector, each showing strategic strengths in their market positions and financial outlooks.
